name: Operations/export-data
description: System/Patient/Group $export conformance tests ported from the Microsoft.Health.Fhir.Shared.Tests.E2E Export suite. Covers kick-off (202 Accepted + Content-Location), request validation failures (400: unsupported resource type/query param, unparsable _since/_till, invalid Accept/Prefer headers, _container and _anonymizationConfig path traversal), status polling (single-shot, not a wait loop), and the cancel lifecycle (DELETE Content-Location -> 202, then 404 on subsequent status/cancel). ExportDataTests.cs and ExportDataValidationTests.cs are entirely SKIPPED: every test in those files creates real data, waits for the export job to reach a terminal 'complete' state, then downloads and parses the resulting ndjson blobs from Azure Storage to compare resource-for-resource with server data -- none of that is possible from a black-box HTTP TestScript run (no polling-until-terminal construct exists in TestScript, and exported blob content cannot be read). Similarly, ExportTests.GivenFhirServer... history/soft-delete/container-content assertions are out of scope for the same reason; only the request/response-shape behavior of ExportTests is represented below.

variable.name: systemExportLocation
variable.description: Content-Location returned by the system-level $export kickoff

variable.name: patientExportLocation
variable.description: Content-Location returned by the Patient/$export kickoff

variable.name: groupExportLocation
variable.description: Content-Location returned by the Group/123456/$export kickoff

variable.name: supportedParamsSystemLocation
variable.description: Content-Location for the system-level export kicked off with _since/_type/_container

variable.name: supportedParamsPatientLocation
variable.description: Content-Location for the Patient-level export kicked off with _since/_type/_container

variable.name: supportedParamsGroupLocation
variable.description: Content-Location for the Group-level export kicked off with _since/_type/_container

variable.name: statusCheckLocation
variable.description: Content-Location used for the immediate status-check test

variable.name: cancelLifecycleLocation
variable.description: Content-Location used for the cancel-then-status-then-cancel-again lifecycle test

test.name: type-level export rejects an unsupported resource type with 400
test.description: Ported from GivenExportIsEnabled_WhenRequestingExportByTypeWithAnInvalidResourceType_ThenServerShouldReturnBadRequest(["Observation/$export", "Patient/id/$export"]). Only Patient (type-level) and Group/{id} (group-level) export routes are supported; both must be rejected synchronously with 400.

operation.description: GET Observation/$export (type-level export is only supported for Patient)

assert.description: Observation/$export must return 400 Bad Request

operation.description: GET Patient/id/$export (id-scoped export is only supported for Group)

assert.description: Patient/id/$export must return 400 Bad Request

test.name: system-level $export kick-off returns 202 Accepted with a non-empty Content-Location
test.description: Ported from GivenExportIsEnabled_WhenRequestingExportWithCorrectHeaders_ThenServerShouldReturnAcceptedAndNonEmptyContentLocationHeader("$export"). Actual export completion/content is not verified (see suite description).

operation.description: GET /$export with Accept: application/fhir+json and Prefer: respond-async

assert.description: System-level export kick-off must return 202 Accepted
Content-Location header must be present and non-empty

operation.description: Best-effort cancel so the job doesn't actually run to completion (mirrors GenerateAndSendCancelExportMessage)

test.name: Patient/$export kick-off returns 202 Accepted with a non-empty Content-Location

operation.description: GET /Patient/$export with Accept: application/fhir+json and Prefer: respond-async

assert.description: Patient-level export kick-off must return 202 Accepted
Content-Location header must be present and non-empty

operation.description: Best-effort cancel so the job doesn't actually run to completion

test.name: Group/{id}/$export kick-off returns 202 Accepted with a non-empty Content-Location
test.description: Ported from GivenExportIsEnabled_WhenRequestingExportWithCorrectHeaders_ThenServerShouldReturnAcceptedAndNonEmptyContentLocationHeader("Group/123456/$export"). The group id is a placeholder (not a real Group) -- like the source test, existence of the group is validated asynchronously during job processing, not at kick-off.

operation.description: GET /Group/123456/$export with Accept: application/fhir+json and Prefer: respond-async

assert.description: Group-level export kick-off must return 202 Accepted
Content-Location header must be present and non-empty

operation.description: Best-effort cancel so the job doesn't actually run to completion

test.name: export kick-off rejects an unsupported query parameter at system, type, and group scope
test.description: Ported from GivenExportIsEnabled_WhenRequestingExportWithUnsupportedQueryParam_ThenServerShouldReturnBadRequest, run against all three export scopes. ValidateExportRequestFilterAttribute rejects any query parameter not on its allow-list.

assert.description: System-level export must return 400 for an unsupported query parameter
Patient-level export must return 400 for an unsupported query parameter
Group-level export must return 400 for an unsupported query parameter

test.name: export kick-off rejects an unparsable _since value
test.description: Ported from GivenUnparsableTime_WhenRequestionExportWithIt_ThenServerShouldReturnBadRequest(_since variants). Both a non-date word and a validly-formatted date with a stray trailing space must fail PartialDateTime binding with 400.

assert.description: Non-date _since value must return 400 Bad Request

operation.description: GET /$export?_type=Patient&_since=2021-06-13T00:00:00Z (with a trailing space, percent-encoded as %20)

assert.description: _since with a trailing space must return 400 Bad Request

test.name: export kick-off rejects an unparsable _till value

assert.description: Non-date _till value must return 400 Bad Request

operation.description: GET /$export?_type=Patient&_till=2021-06-13T00:00:00Z (with a trailing space, percent-encoded as %20)

assert.description: _till with a trailing space must return 400 Bad Request

test.name: export kick-off with supported query parameters (_since, _type, _container) returns 202 Accepted
test.description: Ported from GivenExportIsEnabled_WhenRequestingExportWithSupportedQueryParam_ThenServerShouldReturnAcceptedAndNonEmptyContentLocationHeader, run against all three export scopes.

assert.description: System-level export with supported params must return 202 Accepted
Content-Location header must be present and non-empty
Patient-level export with supported params must return 202 Accepted
Content-Location header must be present and non-empty
Group-level export with supported params must return 202 Accepted
Content-Location header must be present and non-empty

test.name: a single immediate status check right after kick-off returns 202 Accepted
test.description: Ported from GivenExportJobExists_WhenRequestingExportStatus_ThenServerShouldReturnAccepted. This is a single GET, not a wait loop, so it does not depend on completion infra. The 202 assertion is warningOnly because a lab backend could conceivably finish an empty/trivial export before this single follow-up check runs, in which case 200 would be returned instead.

operation.description: GET /$export to create a job record

assert.description: Kick-off must return 202 Accepted

operation.description: GET the Content-Location immediately after kick-off

assert.description: Immediate status check should still return 202 Accepted (warningOnly: the job could complete before this check runs)

test.name: status check for an unknown export job id returns 404 Not Found
test.description: Ported from GivenExportJobDoesNotExist_WhenRequestingExportStatus_ThenServerShouldReturnNotFound. Uses a fixed, presumably-never-issued job id in place of Guid.NewGuid().

operation.description: GET /_operations/export/{id} for a job id that was never created

assert.description: Status check for an unknown job id must return 404 Not Found

test.name: export kick-off rejects invalid Accept header values with 400
test.description: Ported from GivenExportIsEnabled_WhenRequestingExportWithInvalidAcceptHeader_ThenServerShouldReturnBadRequest. ValidateExportRequestFilterAttribute requires exactly one Accept header equal to application/fhir+json.

assert.description: Accept: application/json must return 400 Bad Request

operation.description: GET /$export with Accept: applicaiton/xml (typo preserved from the source test)

assert.description: An unrecognized Accept value must return 400 Bad Request
Accept: */* must return 400 Bad Request

operation.description: GET /$export with an empty Accept header

assert.description: An empty Accept header must return 400 Bad Request

test.name: export kick-off rejects invalid Prefer header values with 400
test.description: Ported from GivenExportIsEnabled_WhenRequestingExportWithInvalidPreferHeader_ThenServerShouldReturnBadRequest. ValidateExportRequestFilterAttribute requires a Prefer header containing respond-async.

operation.description: GET /$export with Prefer: respond-async, wait=10 (wait= is not a recognized token)

assert.description: Prefer: respond-async, wait=10 must return 400 Bad Request
Prefer: respond-status must return 400 Bad Request
Prefer: * must return 400 Bad Request

operation.description: GET /$export with an empty Prefer header

assert.description: An empty Prefer header must return 400 Bad Request

test.name: export kick-off rejects path traversal in the _container parameter
test.description: Ported from GivenExportIsEnabled_WhenContainerContainsPathTraversal_ThenServerShouldReturnBadRequest. The three variants exercise: a raw '/'-separated traversal, a raw '\\'-separated traversal (percent-encoded here as %5C so it survives as a literal backslash), and a double percent-encoded traversal (%252f decodes once to %2f, then again to '/', which is exactly what ExportController's two-pass Uri.UnescapeDataString check is designed to catch).

assert.description: _container=../secret must return 400 Bad Request
_container=..\\secret must return 400 Bad Request
Double-encoded _container traversal must return 400 Bad Request

test.name: export kick-off rejects path traversal in the _anonymizationConfig parameter
test.description: Ported from GivenExportIsEnabled_WhenAnonymizationConfigContainsPathTraversal_ThenServerShouldReturnBadRequest. A valid _container is supplied so the request reaches the anonymization-config path-traversal check (ExportController validates _container before _anonymizationConfig).

assert.description: _anonymizationConfig=../secret/config.json must return 400 Bad Request
Backslash _anonymizationConfig traversal must return 400 Bad Request
Double-encoded _anonymizationConfig traversal must return 400 Bad Request

test.name: cancel lifecycle: create, cancel, status-check, cancel-again
test.description: Ported from GivenExportJobExists_WhenCancelledThenStatusCheckedThenCancelledAgain_ThenCancelReturns202AndSubsequentCallsReturn404 (see https://hl7.org/fhir/uv/bulkdata/STU2/export.html#bulk-data-delete-request). Fully deterministic single-shot HTTP calls, no polling required: kick-off -> 202, DELETE Content-Location -> 202, GET Content-Location -> 404, DELETE Content-Location again -> 404.

operation.description: Step 1: GET /$export to create an export job

assert.description: Step 1: kick-off must return 202 Accepted
Step 1: Content-Location header must be present

operation.description: Step 2: DELETE the Content-Location to cancel the job

assert.description: Step 2: cancelling an in-progress job must return 202 Accepted

operation.description: Step 3: GET the Content-Location after cancellation

assert.description: Step 3: status check after cancellation must return 404 Not Found
Step 4: cancelling an already-cancelled job must return 404 Not Found
